message:
type: SURVEY
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Adding a new get_image_mimetype() method to ImageBlock class that lazily computes/caches image_mimetype; refactoring image_to_base64() validator to use it instead of inline filetype.guess() logic

type: SURVEY
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Adding a new module-level normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes] helper in types.py, and refactoring ImageBlock.image_to_base64() model_validator method to use it. Let me know if you also touch this file/method.

type: DRAFT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: image_to_base64() method + normalize_image_bytes/get_image_mimetype helpers
code: def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Normalize raw or already base64-encoded image bytes.

    Returns a tuple of (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) where
    encoded_image is guaranteed to be base64-encoded, and
    decoded_for_guessing is the raw bytes suitable for MIME type detection.
    """
    try:
        decoded_for_guessing = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
        encoded_image = image
    except Exception:
        encoded_image = base64.b64encode(image)
        decoded_for_guessing = image
    return encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing


class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    ...

    def get_image_mimetype(self, decoded_image: bytes | None = None) -> str | None:
        """Lazily compute and cache the image mimetype.

        Args:
            decoded_image: optional raw (non-base64) bytes to guess from, to
                avoid re-decoding when the caller already has them on hand.
        """
        if self.image_mimetype:
            return self.image_mimetype
        if decoded_image is None:
            if not self.image:
                return None
            decoded_image = base64.b64decode(self.image)
        guess = filetype.guess(decoded_image)
        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing)

        return self
note: Proposed merge: my normalize_image_bytes() handles base64 detection/encoding, your get_image_mimetype() handles lazy mimetype compute+cache. image_to_base64 calls both. Let me know your exact get_image_mimetype signature/body if different so we can converge verbatim.

type: SURVEY
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: image_to_base64() / normalize_image_bytes()
note: We both touch image_to_base64(). Can you share the exact proposed source of normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes] (what each element of the tuple represents, e.g. raw vs base64 vs mimetype-sniff-bytes) and how image_to_base64() calls it? I'm adding ImageBlock.get_image_mimetype() (lazy-computed + cached mimetype resolver using filetype.guess on the RAW bytes) which image_to_base64() will call before b64-encoding. Want to merge cleanly so mimetype guessing still happens pre-encoding.

type: DRAFT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: image_to_base64() method + normalize_image_bytes/get_image_mimetype helpers
code: def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Normalize raw or already base64-encoded image bytes.

    Returns a tuple of (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) where
    encoded_image is guaranteed to be base64-encoded, and
    decoded_for_guessing is the raw (non-base64) bytes suitable for MIME
    type detection via filetype.guess().
    """
    try:
        decoded_for_guessing = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
        encoded_image = image
    except Exception:
        encoded_image = base64.b64encode(image)
        decoded_for_guessing = image
    return encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing


class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    ...

    def get_image_mimetype(self, decoded_image: bytes | None = None) -> str | None:
        """Lazily compute and cache the image mimetype.

        Args:
            decoded_image: optional raw (non-base64) bytes to guess from, to
                avoid re-decoding when the caller already has them on hand.
        """
        if self.image_mimetype:
            return self.image_mimetype
        if decoded_image is None:
            if not self.image:
                return None
            decoded_image = base64.b64decode(self.image)
        guess = filetype.guess(decoded_image)
        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing)

        return self
note: Resending in case this crossed with your SURVEY: tuple is (encoded_image=guaranteed-base64 bytes, decoded_for_guessing=raw bytes for filetype.guess). normalize_image_bytes detects if input is already base64 (tries b64decode with validate=True) so it doesn't double-encode; otherwise it encodes raw bytes. image_to_base64 calls normalize_image_bytes first (so mimetype guessing uses decoded_for_guessing, still pre-storing since self.image is only reassigned after), then calls your get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing) passing the already-decoded bytes so it does not need to re-decode self.image. Does this match your get_image_mimetype signature/body? Please send AGREE with exact code if so, or DRAFT back with corrections.
